description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with two stages: build and upload. In the build stage add a job named build-vs2015-win2019-image that runs on a Windows 2019 Docker shell runner (tag cunity-windows-2019-docker-shell) and executes the exact commands: set IMAGE=registry.gitlab.com/%CI_PROJECT_PATH%/win2019-vs2015:%CI_COMMIT_REF_NAME%; docker login -u gitlab-ci-token -p %CI_JOB_TOKEN% registry.gitlab.com; docker build --no-cache --tag %IMAGE% . -f win2019-vs2015\Dockerfile; docker login -u gitlab-ci-token -p %CI_JOB_TOKEN% registry.gitlab.com; docker run --rm -w c:\vc -v %CD%\validation_cmake:c:\vc %IMAGE% build.bat; docker push %IMAGE%. In the upload stage add a job named publish-vs2015-win2019-image that runs only on the master branch, also on the same runner, and runs: set IMAGE=registry.gitlab.com/%CI_PROJECT_PATH%/win2019-vs2015:%CI_COMMIT_REF_NAME%; set PUBLIC=cunity/windows-2019-vs2015-cmake:%CI_COMMIT_REF_NAME%; docker login -u gitlab-ci-token -p %CI_JOB_TOKEN% registry.gitlab.com; docker pull %IMAGE%; docker tag %IMAGE% %PUBLIC%; docker login -u %DOCKER_HUB_USERNAME% -p %DOCKER_HUB_PASSWORD% docker.io; docker push %PUBLIC%.
